description: Second-pass implementation completeness contract consolidating every mandatory Workflow System V1 requirement into Ralph-sized implementation and validation stories.

userStories.title: Create packages/workflows module layout
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I need the required package structure so that all workflow capabilities have clear homes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/workflows with submodules or direct equivalents for api, registry, types, validation, graph, compiler, runtime, store, xstate, fixtures, and testing
Each submodule owns the responsibility described in the completeness contract
Package exports are minimal and do not expose runtime internals through authoring APIs
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define public authoring API and utility types
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I need the complete authoring API so that TypeScript workflows can be created, registered, validated, compiled, serialized, projected, and resolved.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose setupWorkflow, workflow, defineWorkflow, provideWorkflow, registerWorkflow, registerWorkflowHandler, registerWorkflowAdapter, registerWorkflowGuard, registerWorkflowHumanAction, registerPluginWorkflows, text, json, template, fixedProfile, validateWorkflow, compileWorkflow, serializeWorkflowForDebug, projectToXState, and resolveWorkflowDefinition or equivalent names
Expose utility types tied to workflow and node ports for input, output, and snapshot values
The API supports both object-first and builder definitions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement canonical JSON-serializable Workflow IR
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I need the canonical IR complete so that definitions can be validated, compiled, debugged, snapshotted, and projected.
acceptanceCriteria: WorkflowDefinition includes id, version, optional name/description, input, output, nodes, edges, initial, final, state, retry, ui, and metadata fields
IR is JSON-serializable and stores refs/ids instead of inline closures
IR is not marketed as V1 workflow YAML/JSON file import/export product support
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement final/completion semantics and V1 ports
userStories.description: As a workflow author, I need explicit start and completion rules so that workflow output is deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: initial or builder startAt defines one or more entry nodes
final or builder doneFrom defines terminal node(s) or output mapping
Terminal node output becomes workflow output unless a final mapper is declared
Workflow output validates against text or JSON output port before run completion
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement all V1 node definition shapes
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I need all node kinds in the IR so that workflow definitions can represent V1 behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Node definitions support agent, code, workflow, adapter, and human kinds
Agent nodes require kind agent, runtime pibo, and fixed profile selection in V1
Code nodes reference registered TypeScript handlers; nested workflow nodes reference workflow id/version; adapter nodes reference registered adapters; human nodes define prompt/schema/timeout
Base nodes support label, input, output, reads, and writes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement edge, adapter, join, merge, and retry IR
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I need complete edge semantics so that composition, routing, state, and retries are explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: Edges support id, from, to, kind, event, condition/guard, join, map, adapter, state mapping, and UI metadata
Edge adapters use kind edgeAdapter with declared output port and registered transform ref
Join policies include all_success, one_success, none_failed_min_one_success, and all_done
Merge policies include replace, append, shallowMerge, and custom handler
Retry policies include maxAttempts, backoff, and optional retryOn with node/adapter > workflow > system default inheritance
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Workflow Registry completeness
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I need registry resolution for all implementation refs so that runtime never executes unknown code.
acceptanceCriteria: Registry registers/resolves workflows, code handlers, adapter handlers, guard handlers, prompt builders, prompt assets, human actions, routing hints, runtime capabilities, custom merge handlers, and plugin-provided capabilities
registerPluginWorkflows supports plugin registration
Validation fails missing statically resolvable refs before execution
Runtime DB stores workflow id, version, definition hash, optional compiled snapshot, and facts while TypeScript source stays in code/registry
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement structured validation diagnostics and rejection rules
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I need validation to reject invalid workflows with actionable diagnostics.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation returns diagnostics with code, message, optional path/nodeId/edgeId, severity, and hint
Error families cover definition, graph, interface, execution, retry-exhausted, adapter, and node-executor errors

Validator rejects malformed ids, missing refs, invalid port payloads, unsupported JSON schemas, incompatible edges, missing handlers/adapters/guards/profiles/workflows, ambiguous joins/guards, undeclared writes, merge conflicts, unbounded cycles, and unbounded back-edges
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement graph store and compiler execution plan
userStories.description: As a runtime developer, I need graph utilities and compiled plans so that runtime executes normalized workflows only.
acceptanceCriteria: Graph store provides stable node/edge ids, has/get/set/update/drop methods, successors, predecessors, inEdges, outEdges, export, copy, and project or equivalents
Graph serialization is deterministic and mutation utilities support debug/test payloads

Compiler produces execution plan with definition identity, normalized nodes/edges, initial/terminal nodes, loop metadata, join metadata, state policy, queue seeds, checkpoint namespaces, and XState projection metadata
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement durable runtime kernel statuses and loop
userStories.description: As a runtime developer, I need boundary-persistent execution so that workflows resume safely after restart.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime executes only compiled plans
Workflow statuses include pending, running, waiting, failed, completed, and cancelled
Node attempt statuses include pending, leased, running, waiting, retry_scheduled, failed, completed, skipped, and cancelled or equivalent skipped projection
Execution loop persists workflow start, node attempt start/result, edge transfer, wait, retry scheduling, failure, cancellation, checkpoint, and completion at transactional boundaries
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement runnable rules, guards, retries, leases, and idempotency
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need deterministic scheduling rules so that workers do not duplicate or lose work.
acceptanceCriteria: Node is runnable only when input exists, dependencies are satisfied, guards allow execution, loop policy permits another iteration, availableAt is due, and no active lease is owned by another worker
Guard results affecting durable routing are persisted or diagnosed
Retry decisions are deterministic over policy, attempt, error, and time with fixed/linear/exponential/none backoff support
Leases prevent duplicate active ownership, heartbeat when supported, expire, and can be reclaimed
Node attempts use deterministic idempotency keys
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement workflow store logical records
userStories.description: As a storage engineer, I need the workflow DB schema so that every runtime fact is inspectable and replayable.
acceptanceCriteria: Store uses pibo-workflows.sqlite or equivalent workflow-specific store for workflow execution facts only

Logical records include workflow_definition_snapshots, workflow_runs, workflow_events, workflow_node_attempts, workflow_edge_transfers, workflow_checkpoints, workflow_wakeups, workflow_wait_tokens, and workflow_human_actions with required fields or equivalents
Store links workflow runs to owner scope, parent run/attempt, Pibo session, project, environment, status, input/output/state/current, and timestamps
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement all node executors against shared attempt model
userStories.description: As a runtime developer, I need complete executor contracts so that every node kind behaves consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent executor resolves fixed profile, obeys policy, routes through normal Pibo Runtime, builds prompts, validates output, records session/trace/effective metadata, and supports cancellation/timeout where possible
Code executor loads registered handler, provides scoped context, enforces reads/writes, validates output/patches, and rejects undeclared writes
Nested workflow executor creates child run, waits for completion/failure, maps output, links parent/child events, and isolates child state by default
Adapter executor resolves deterministic adapter, validates source/output/target, and persists adapter attempts/errors
Human executor creates durable waits, validates actions, records decisions, and routes outcomes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement commands, waits, wakeups, and human actions
userStories.description: As a workflow author, I need executor commands and waits to be durable so that external and human interactions survive restart.
acceptanceCriteria: Commands include goto, update, resume, requestHumanInput, emitArtifact, cancel, complete, fail, and handoff or equivalents and are persisted before application
Command rules validate targets, state writes, resume values, artifacts, completion output, errors, and dynamic routing permission
Wait kinds include human input, child workflow completion, timeout, external signal, scheduled retry, and Pibo Runtime response or equivalents
Human actions include approve, reject, resume, cancel, custom registry actions, schema validation, and wakeup correlation data
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement CLI/debug and Project UI surfaces
userStories.description: As a full-stack developer, I need the required operator and user surfaces so that workflows can be inspected and controlled.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI/debug exposes workflow list, validate, run, inspect, approve, reject, resume, and cancel equivalents; No XState CLI command is added in V1
Project UI shows workflow-backed sessions/runs, workflow id/version/status, current node/state, node status list, final output where permitted, diagnostics, waiting reason, and human actions
Dedicated Workflow/XState Web UI tab visualizes from Pibo/XState projection
Full visual workflow creation/editing is not included in V1
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement XState projection and inspection contract
userStories.description: As a UI/runtime engineer, I need XState integration without making it durable truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Projection maps workflow definition, run status, nodes, all node kinds, edges, guards, waits, retry delays, state projection, and terminal states to XState-compatible config/snapshots
Snapshot kinds kernel, xstate, and ui are distinguished and versioned when persisted
Define PiboWorkflowActor interface with start/send/stop/getSnapshot and optional persist/restore/inspect

Inspection events cover exact families including @pibo.workflow.actor.created, @pibo.workflow.event.sent, @pibo.workflow.transition, @pibo.workflow.snapshot, @pibo.workflow.action, @pibo.workflow.child.output, @pibo.workflow.wait.entered, and @pibo.workflow.wait.resumed
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement execution environment and capability validation
userStories.description: As a security reviewer, I need environment and capability checks so that workflow execution respects isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow/node environment policy supports inherit, host, worktree, docker-worker, and remote with inherit default
Effective environment is stored for audit/debug and passed to executors through scoped context
Cleanup/cancellation accounts for environment-owned resources

Capability validation checks profile existence, tools/skills/context availability, structured output support or fallback, session resume, timeout, budget, isolation, environment policy, and known concurrency limits when possible
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preserve compatibility with Pibo sessions and related capabilities
userStories.description: As a Pibo user, I need normal sessions to keep working so that workflows are additive.
acceptanceCriteria: Normal Pibo Sessions work without workflow configuration
simple-chat can be treated as a workflow wrapper when workflow selection is enabled
Workflow runs link to Pibo Sessions and Project Sessions
pibo-event-contract includes workflow run, node, transition, wait, failure, and completion events
Advanced LangGraph-style channels, generalized Pregel/superstep runtime, and pending writes for partial supersteps remain deferred from V1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add required fixtures and automated tests
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I need complete fixtures and tests so that every V1 contract has a pass/fail gate.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixtures include minimal one-node pibo-agent, mixed workflow, adapter edge, human wait/resume, registry/plugin, debug serialization, nested workflow, and bounded back-edge/review-loop workflows

Tests cover malformed definitions, ports, edges, adapters, all node kinds, profile resolution, prompts, state isolation/merge conflicts, bounded loops, XState snapshots, persistence statuses, restart boundaries, stale leases, nested child failure, and UI/CLI control
Required checks include npm run typecheck, workflow unit tests, workflow persistence/restart tests, manual one-node pibo-agent workflow, manual adapter composition, and manual bounded review/fix loop
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add task traceability and release gate documentation
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I need task traceability so that incomplete implementations can be rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: Document completion gates for Foundation, Interfaces, First-class runtime unit, Edges, Adapters, Node kinds, Agent profile/routing, Prompts, State/backtracking, XState, Persistence, UI/Inspection, Documentation, and Validation task groups
Release gates cover MVP, V1 core, V1 product, V1 release, and V2 candidates
Implementation can be rejected when any MUST item in the completeness contract is missing
Typecheck passes
